intro: Benthamantha mollis, also known as Borreria mollis, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Benthamantha mollis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
